sentences.text: A) The printing press, invented by Johannes Gutenberg around 1440, revolutionised the spread of information across Europe.
B) Before its invention, books were copied by hand, making them expensive and rare.
C) The press enabled mass production of texts, dramatically lowering their cost.
D) As literacy rates rose, new ideas in science, religion, and politics spread rapidly, contributing to the Renaissance and Reformation.
A) The discovery of penicillin by Alexander Fleming in 1928 marked the beginning of the antibiotic era.
B) Fleming noticed that a mould had contaminated one of his petri dishes and was killing the surrounding bacteria.
C) He identified the mould as Penicillium notatum and named the antibacterial substance it produced penicillin.
D) Further development by Florey and Chain in the 1940s transformed penicillin into a widely used medicine that saved millions of lives.
A) Ocean acidification is the ongoing decrease in the pH of Earth's oceans, primarily caused by the absorption of carbon dioxide from the atmosphere.
B) Since the industrial revolution, the average pH of surface ocean waters has fallen from 8.2 to approximately 8.1.
C) This change may appear small, but pH is measured on a logarithmic scale, meaning the oceans are now around thirty percent more acidic than they were.
D) The consequences for marine life, particularly shell-forming organisms such as oysters, clams, and corals, are increasingly severe.
A) Electric vehicles are often presented as a cleaner alternative to conventional petrol and diesel cars.
B) They produce no tailpipe emissions, which can improve air quality in densely populated cities.
C) However, their overall environmental impact still depends on how the electricity used to charge them is generated.
D) For this reason, transport electrification is most effective when combined with a shift toward renewable energy.
